name: Dark Pumpernickel Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water.
2. Stir in molasses, salt, oil, caraway seeds, rye flour, shredded wheat cereal, and cocoa until smooth.
3. Gradually stir in the remaining flour; turn dough onto a floured surface.
4. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
5. Knead dough until smooth.
6. Shape dough into two rounds and place in a greased large bowl, turning to grease the top.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
8. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
9. Grease a cookie sheet with margarine and sprinkle with cornmeal.
10. Punch down dough, divide into two rounds, shape each into slightly flat loaves, and place on the prepared cookie sheet.
11. Brush tops lightly with margarine.
12. Let rise until doubled in size, about 50 minutes.
13. Bake for 35 minutes until hollow when tapped, then remove from sheet and cool. Makes 2 loaves.
